subject: [PATCH v2 12/18] gfs2: convert iomap ops to ->iomap_next()

2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch converts the GFS2 file system's iomap operations to use the new ->iomap_next() callback, which is a helper function that finishes previous mappings and produces the next one. This change does not introduce any functional changes, it only updates the interface used by the file system. The conversion uses the iomap_process() helper function to manage the mapping process.

reviews.author: Andreas Gruenbacher
reviews.summary: Raised a question about the repository and branch used in the patch, specifically asking for clarification on which branch the changes were made against.
reviews.raw_body:
Joanne,

you say in the cover letter that

>This series is submitted against Christian's vfs iomap tree, and is based
> on top of the current head of his tree (commit f2727952b21d1).

which repository and branch is that? It's not vfs.iomap in
git://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/vfs/vfs.git; I don't
have a proper merge point.

Thanks,
Andreas

reviews.author: Joanne Koong
reviews.summary: Provided clarification on the repository and branch used in the patch, including a link to a GitHub repository with the changes.
reviews.raw_body:
On Fri, Jul 3, 2026 at 4:45 AM Andreas Gruenbacher <agruenba@redhat.com> wrote:
>
> Joanne,
>
> you say in the cover letter that
>
> >This series is submitted against Christian's vfs iomap tree, and is based
> > on top of the current head of his tree (commit f2727952b21d1).
>
> which repository and branch is that? It's not vfs.iomap in
> git://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/vfs/vfs.git; I don't
> have a proper merge point.

Hi Andreas,

This is on the vfs-7.3.iomap branch [1] on the vfs tree. This was on
top of commit f2727952b21d1 ("iomap: add a separate bio_set for
iomap_split_ioend") but it looks like that branch has since been
rebased, as I don't see that commit hash there anymore. If it makes it
easier, I've pushed the changes to a github repo [2] that's on top of
v7.2-rc1 in the main linux tree. Sorry for the confusion, I'll include
a link to a pullable branch in future versions.

Thanks,
Joanne

[1] https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/vfs/vfs.git/log/?h=vfs-7.3.iomap
[2] https://github.com/joannekoong/linux/commits/iomap_iter_next_v2/

2026-07-03.patch_summary: This patch converts the gfs2 iomap operations to use the new ->iomap_next() callback, which is a part of the iomap_process helper. This change does not introduce any functional changes and only updates the iomap ops structure.
